description: Focuses on the physical, mental, and social well-being of the people, aiming to deliver a healthy environment, world-class infrastructure, and a transparent, corruption-free government.

key_promises: Providing free IVF (In Vitro Fertilization) treatments for childless couples in government hospitals.
Modernizing village hospitals with dialysis units, 4-bed ICUs, maternity labs, operation theaters, and rapid ambulances.
Offering high-quality medicines at 50% to 90% discounted rates through government pharmacies.
Launching the 'Happy Tamil Nadu Mission' to improve mental health, social harmony, and overall quality of life.
Implementing a Mental Health Medical Scheme with dedicated psychologists in communities and schools to address mental stress.
Establishing Tamil Nadu as a global identity for Siddha medicine, creating a Siddha University, and connecting hubs like Thiruvannamalai, Chidambaram, and Kutralam.
Allocating a ₹3,000 Crore Climate Budget to combat climate change and attract green investments.
Establishing an advanced early warning disaster management system, including 5 coastal radars and 38 district thermal cameras.
Creating a Sustainable Energy Economic Division under the Chief Minister's office to monitor carbon emissions and enforce green schemes.
Increasing Tamil Nadu's forest cover to 33% by 2031 through the 'Green Tamil Nadu Scheme', planting native trees like Sandalwood and Teak.
Implementing a strict zero-tolerance policy against discharging industrial waste into rivers, and establishing eco-parks along the Cauvery and Thamirabarani rivers.
Installing 50 km of solar fencing in conflict zones like Nilgiris and Coimbatore, and providing ₹10 Lakh compensation for wildlife-related deaths.
Protecting mangrove forests and removing encroachments from the Pallikaranai marshland.
Allocating a ₹500 Crore fund to combat severe air pollution in major cities.
Restoring the Cooum and Adyar rivers on a 'Mission-mode' basis.
Ensuring 24/7 uninterrupted electricity across Tamil Nadu by resolving overloaded feeders.
Providing substantial solar subsidies for households: ₹35,000 for 1kW, ₹70,000 for 2kW, and ₹1 Lakh for 3kW+.
Implementing a monthly electricity billing cycle, replacing the current bi-monthly system.
Reforming TANGEDCO to reduce AT&C losses from 16% to 10% and lowering electricity charges for apartments and MSMEs.
Operating 50% of public transport (5000 TNSTC buses) on renewable energy by 2031.
Expediting Chennai Metro Phase 2, starting Coimbatore Metro Phase 1, and preparing the DPR for Madurai Metro.
Expanding night services and cargo handling at airports through government-private partnerships.
Pushing for railway connectivity projects, including the 4th line from Tambaram to Chengalpattu, and connecting unserved regions.
Establishing multi-modal transit hubs integrating roads, rail, and air transport to reduce logistics costs by 20-30%.
Implementing AI-driven traffic management and offering 'Good Samaritan' awards for helping accident victims.
Allocating ₹20,000 Crore annually for pothole-free, well-maintained state and village highways.
Initiating ferry services to Sri Lanka and Malaysia to revive ancient Tamil sea trade routes.
Increasing the MGNREGA rural employment guarantee from 100 to 150 days.
Developing 5,000 'Model Villages' equipped with modern urban infrastructure, schools, and hospitals.
Creating 'Village Industry Clusters' to integrate local MSMEs and startups with supply chains.
Achieving 'Slum-Free Cities' by 2031, providing proper housing, drainage, and basic rights.
Protecting cultural cities (Thanjavur, Madurai, Kanchipuram, Mahabalipuram) with heritage conservation master plans.
Implementing a 'Climate-Resilient TN Master Plan 2045' to manage flood risks in vulnerable cities like Chennai, Thoothukudi, and Kanyakumari.
Reducing the severe property tax hikes previously imposed by the DMK government.
Introducing India's first 'No-Application' system where eligible citizens automatically receive welfare scheme benefits directly.
Implementing a Right to Service Act ensuring over 500 government services (certificates, patta, etc.) are delivered within a guaranteed timeframe.
Launching the 'Vettri Tamil Nadu Super App' to deliver birth certificates, ration cards, and voter IDs directly to mobile phones.
Establishing a 'Real-Time Governance Dashboard' for citizens to track government revenue, expenditures, and project statuses.
Creating 'Makkal Arangam', a digital platform where petitions gathering 10,000 signatures require direct government response, and 5 Lakh signatures prompt a legislative assembly debate.
Allocating 10% of district development funds to projects selected directly by citizens' votes.
Setting up a dedicated anti-corruption helpline and enacting a 'State Whistleblower Protection Act'.
Strengthening the Lokayukta to proactively investigate corruption charges against elected officials and public servants.
Abolishing sand smuggling through a transparent, online auction system.
Creating a 'Drug-Free Tamil Nadu' by introducing stringent laws to confiscate assets of drug peddlers and establishing a dedicated anti-drug force.
Closing TASMAC (liquor) shops in a phased manner and establishing rehabilitation centers to create a healthy, non-alcoholic state.
Ensuring independent investigations and severe legal action against officers involved in custodial deaths.
Publishing 5 crucial special financial reports covering Social Justice, Regional Development, Culture, Education, and Environment.
Releasing a 'White Paper' on State Finances to expose current debt levels and prevent further economic mismanagement.
Establishing the 'Tamil Nadu Strategic Investment Authority' (TNSIA) to bypass red tape and drive investments in AI, EV, Aerospace, and Semiconductors.
Restructuring Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) to eliminate losses and introduce professional management practices.
